commands.review_summary:
The PR defers pasteboard writes and copy-feedback state changes from two hosted menu click handlers, avoids redundant overlay updates, and adds AppKit overlay tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main contains the synchronous animation and pasteboard operations identified by the concrete macOS 26.4.1 steps in the linked report. This read-only review did not independently execute the macOS reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Require contributor-supplied built-app proof and removal of the release-owned changelog edit before normal maintainer merge review; automation cannot supply proof from the contributor's affected setup.

target.title: fix(menu): defer copy-icon click work off the NSMenu tracking loop (macOS 26 beachball)
target.body:
## Problem

On macOS 26.4.1 clicking the `doc.on.doc` "Copy error" icon next to an error in a hosted menu card freezes the cursor for several seconds. Reproduces deterministically with any provider whose card surfaces an error (e.g. Claude web `Unauthorized`).

Two SwiftUI / NSView handlers run work synchronously inside the live `NSMenu` tracking event loop:

1. **`CopyIconButton.body`** ([MenuCardView.swift:316](https://github.com/steipete/CodexBar/blob/main/Sources/CodexBar/MenuCardView.swift#L316-L351)) — `Button` action calls `withAnimation { didCopy = true }` immediately and queues a second `withAnimation { didCopy = false }` 0.9s later. Each `withAnimation` inside a tracking-mode hosted view forces a synchronous SwiftUI hosting layout pass that the menu engine cannot service mid-tracking.
2. **`ClickToCopyView.mouseDown`** ([ClickToCopyOverlay.swift](https://github.com/steipete/CodexBar/blob/main/Sources/CodexBar/ClickToCopyOverlay.swift)) — `NSPasteboard.general.setString` runs synchronously. Pasteboard writes emit distributed notifications whose synchronous watchers can re-enter the menu engine.

This is the same class of bug Peter has been chasing in 0.32.5/0.32.6 menu perf work (#1352 throwaway hosting, #1291 closed-menu rebuilds, #1297 icon observation churn, #1303 quota indicator constraint churn) — the copy-icon hotspots weren't in radar.

Fixes #1388.

## Solution

Both handlers now defer their post-click work off the current menu-tracking tick:

1. `CopyIconButton.handleCopy()` (new helper) — `DispatchQueue.main.async`s the pasteboard write, `didCopy = true`, and the 0.9s reset task. Drops `withAnimation` for plain state mutation (the icon swap is fine without animation; on a fast menu click the user only sees the new icon).
2. `ClickToCopyView.mouseDown` — same pattern, captures `copyText` locally and `DispatchQueue.main.async`s the `NSPasteboard.setString` call. `updateNSView` gains a no-op guard so a stable parent card re-render doesn't churn the `NSView`.

Strictly in-place patches. No `MenuDescriptor` plumbing change, no API change, no new public types. UX preserved — clicking the icon next to the error still copies; the checkmark flips on the next tick and reverts after 0.9s.

## Testing

`Tests/CodexBarTests/ClickToCopyOverlayTests.swift` (new):

- `view stores the latest copyText`
- `mouseDown writes copyText to general pasteboard asynchronously` (synthesizes an `NSEvent` from `CGEvent`, asserts a sentinel string appears on the general pasteboard after a 50ms yield)
- `accepts first mouse so error text overlay is clickable on first focus`

```
swift test --filter ClickToCopyOverlayTests   # 3/3 pass
./Scripts/lint.sh lint                         # 0 violations / 1025 files
swift build                                    # clean
```

Verified locally on macOS 26.4.1 — the Copy-error icon click is now instantaneous, no beachball, no system freeze.

## Out of scope / follow-ups

- The deeper "promote copy to a dedicated `NSMenuItem` via the existing `MenuDescriptor.MenuAction.copyError` case + `@objc copyError(_:)` handler" rewrite (cc-claude Option C) would *remove* the SwiftUI in-menu click path entirely. That's the cleaner long-term answer if more in-menu copy affordances appear, but it changes UX (a separate menu item replaces the inline icon) — left for a follow-up if these handlers prove fragile again on future macOS releases.
- The pattern likely applies anywhere else SwiftUI buttons hosted inside `NSMenu` tracking call `withAnimation` or write to `NSPasteboard` synchronously. Worth a sweep when bandwidth permits.
